chunks:
# ==============================================================================
# IVD Recipe: Background Job Pattern
# ==============================================================================
# Version: 1.0
# Created: 2026-01-23
# Category: infrastructure_patterns
# ==============================================================================

recipe:
  name: "Background Job Pattern"
  version: "1.0"
  category: "infrastructure_patterns"
  created: "2026-01-23"
  author: "Leo Celis"
  
  description: |
    Standard pattern for building background jobs using Celery for asynchronous
    task processing. This pattern ensures jobs are reliable, monitorable, and
    handle failures gracefully. Suitable for any long-running or async operation
    that shouldn't block user-facing requests.
    
    Key characteristics:
    - Asynchronous execution (doesn't block caller)
    - Retryable with exponential backoff
    - Monitored for failures and performance
    - Idempotent (safe to retry)
    - Progress tracking for long-running jobs
  
  applies_to:
    - "Long-running operations (> 5 seconds)"
    - "Batch processing of multiple items"
    - "Operations that can be asynchronous"
    - "Tasks that need retry logic"
    - "Scheduled/periodic tasks"
  
  when_to_use:
    - "Operation takes > 5 seconds (would timeout in HTTP request)"
    - "Operation doesn't need immediate user feedback"
    - "Operation should retry on transient failures"
    - "Operation processes large batches"
    - "Operation should run on schedule (cron-like)"
  
  when_NOT_to_use:
    - "Operation must complete synchronously (< 1 second response needed)"
    - "Operation requires immediate user feedback"
    - "Operation is simple enough for inline processing"
    - "Infrastructure overhead not justified by use case"

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# TEMPLATE SECTIONS
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

intent_template:
  summary: "[Domain] background job for [purpose]"
  goal: "Process [items] asynchronously to [outcome] without blocking [caller]"
  success_metric: "Job completion rate >= [95-99]%, processing time < [target]"
  stakeholders:
    - "[Primary stakeholder who cares about job completion]"
    - "[Operations team managing infrastructure]"

constraints_template:
  - name: "job_completion_rate"
    requirement: "successful completion >= [95-99]%"
    typical_values: "95% minimum, 98% good, 99%+ excellent"
    test: "tests/test_[job_name]_reliability.py::test_completion_rate"
    consequence_if_violated: "[Items] not processed, [business impact]"
    
  - name: "processing_time"
    requirement: "p95 processing time < [target based on SLA]"
    typical_values: "Depends on job complexity and SLA"
    test: "tests/test_[job_name]_performance.py::test_processing_time"
    consequence_if_violated: "SLA breach, delayed [downstream process]"
    
  - name: "retry_behavior"
    requirement: "max retries = [3-5 typical], exponential backoff"
    typical_values: "3-5 retries with exponential backoff (2^retry * base_delay)"
    test: "tests/test_[job_name]_retry.py::test_retry

reliability.py::test_completion_rate"
    consequence_if_violated: "[Items] not processed, [business impact]"
    
  - name: "processing_time"
    requirement: "p95 processing time < [target based on SLA]"
    typical_values: "Depends on job complexity and SLA"
    test: "tests/test_[job_name]_performance.py::test_processing_time"
    consequence_if_violated: "SLA breach, delayed [downstream process]"
    
  - name: "retry_behavior"
    requirement: "max retries = [3-5 typical], exponential backoff"
    typical_values: "3-5 retries with exponential backoff (2^retry * base_delay)"
    test: "tests/test_[job_name]_retry.py::test_retry_logic"
    consequence_if_violated: "Jobs fail permanently on transient errors or overwhelm system with retries"
    
  - name: "idempotency"
    requirement: "job can be retried safely without side effects"
    typical_values: "Must be idempotent (same result if run multiple times)"
    test: "tests/test_[job_name]_idempotency.py::test_multiple_runs"
    consequence_if_violated: "Duplicate processing, data corruption"

rationale_template:
  decision: "Use Celery background job for [specific task]"
  reasoning: |
    - Task duration ([X seconds]) exceeds synchronous threshold
    - Asynchronous processing improves user experience
    - Retry logic handles transient failures
    - [Domain-specific reasoning]
  evidence: "playground/[job_name]_performance_analysis_[date].ipynb"
  stakeholder: "[Role] ([Name])"
  date: "[YYYY-MM-DD]"

alternatives_template:
  - name: "synchronous_processing"
    typical_rejection_reason: "Task duration too long, would cause timeouts or poor UX"
    when_it_works: "Task completes in < 2 seconds reliably"
    
  - name: "lambda_functions"
    typical_rejection_reason: "Cold start latency, 15-minute timeout limit, stateless"
    when_it_works: "Short-lived, stateless, event-driven tasks"
    
  - name: "cron_job"
    typical_rejection_reason: "Needs event-driven execution, not scheduled"
    when_it_works: "Purely time-based schedule, no event triggers"
    
  - name: "message_queue_direct"
    typical_rejection_reason: "Need worker management, monitoring, retry logic (Celery provides this)"
    when_it_works: "Simple queue consumer, no advanced features needed"

risks_template:
  - condition: "If job completion rate < [95]%"
    impact: "high"
    action: "Alert ops team, investigate failures, review error logs"
    monitor: "Celery Flower dashboard / CloudWatch metrics"
    severity: "high"
    
  - condition: "If queue depth > [threshold]"
    impact: "medium"
    action: "Scale workers, investigate performance bottleneck"
    monitor: "Redis queue depth metrics"
    severity: "medium"
    
  - condition: "If job duration increases significantly"
    impact: "medium"
    action: "Profile job execution, optimize slow operations"
    monitor: "APM job duration metrics"
    severity: "medium"
    
  - condition: "If jobs create cascading failures"
    impact: "high"
    action: "Implement circuit breaker, rate limiting"
    monitor: "Error rate and retry metrics"
    severity: "high"

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# IMPLEMENTATION PATTE

ium"
    action: "Scale workers, investigate performance bottleneck"
    monitor: "Redis queue depth metrics"
    severity: "medium"
    
  - condition: "If job duration increases significantly"
    impact: "medium"
    action: "Profile job execution, optimize slow operations"
    monitor: "APM job duration metrics"
    severity: "medium"
    
  - condition: "If jobs create cascading failures"
    impact: "high"
    action: "Implement circuit breaker, rate limiting"
    monitor: "Error rate and retry metrics"
    severity: "high"

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# IMPLEMENTATION PATTERN
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

implementation_pattern:
  directory_structure: |
    job/[job_name]/
    ├── [job_name]_job.py         # Main job definition
    ├── [job_name]_intent.yaml    # Intent artifact (uses this recipe)
    ├── processor.py               # Core processing logic
    ├── utils.py                   # Helper functions
    └── tests/
        ├── test_job_execution.py  # Basic job execution tests
        ├── test_reliability.py    # Completion rate, retry tests
        ├── test_performance.py    # Processing time tests
        └── test_idempotency.py    # Idempotency tests
  
  code_structure: |
    # [job_name]_job.py
    from your_app.celery_app import celery
    from job.[job_name].processor import Processor
    
    @celery.task(
        bind=True,
        name='job.[job_name]',
        max_retries=3,
        default_retry_delay=60,  # 1 minute
        autoretry_for=(TransientException,),
        retry_backoff=True,
        retry_backoff_max=600,  # 10 minutes
        retry_jitter=True
    )
    def process_[job_name](self, item_id: int):
        """
        Process [item] asynchronously.
        
        Intent: [job_name]_intent.yaml
        Constraints: completion_rate >= 95%, idempotent
        """
        processor = Processor()
        
        try:
            # 1. Fetch item (with idempotency check)
            item = processor.fetch_item(item_id)
            if processor.already_processed(item_id):
                return {'status': 'already_processed', 'item_id': item_id}
            
            # 2. Process item
            result = processor.process(item)
            
            # 3. Mark as processed (idempotency)
            processor.mark_processed(item_id, result)
            
            # 4. Track metrics
            processor.track_success(item_id)
            
            return {'status': 'success', 'item_id': item_id, 'result': result}
            
        except TransientException as e:
            # Auto-retry handled by decorator
            processor.track_retry(item_id, str(e))
            raise
            
        except PermanentException as e:
            # Don't retry, log and fail
            processor.track_failure(item_id, str(e))
            return {'status': 'failed', 'item_id': item_id, 'error': str(e)}
  
  key_files:
    - file: "[job_name]_job.py"
      purpose: "Celery task definition with retry logic"
      key_functions:
        - name: "process_[job_name]"
          purpose: "Main task function,

uccess', 'item_id': item_id, 'result': result}
            
        except TransientException as e:
            # Auto-retry handled by decorator
            processor.track_retry(item_id, str(e))
            raise
            
        except PermanentException as e:
            # Don't retry, log and fail
            processor.track_failure(item_id, str(e))
            return {'status': 'failed', 'item_id': item_id, 'error': str(e)}
  
  key_files:
    - file: "[job_name]_job.py"
      purpose: "Celery task definition with retry logic"
      key_functions:
        - name: "process_[job_name]"
          purpose: "Main task function, orchestrates processing"
          
    - file: "processor.py"
      purpose: "Core business logic for processing"
      key_functions:
        - name: "process"
          purpose: "Execute core processing logic"
        - name: "already_processed"
          purpose: "Check if item already processed (idempotency)"
        - name: "mark_processed"
          purpose: "Record successful processing"
          
  dependencies:
    - "celery (task queue framework)"
    - "redis (message broker and result backend)"
    - "prometheus_client (for metrics)"

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# VERIFICATION PATTERN
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

verification_pattern:
  continuous_checks:
    - check: "Job completion rate"
      frequency: "hourly"
      command: "Monitor Celery Flower metrics or Prometheus"
      threshold: ">= [constraint from intent]"
      
    - check: "Queue depth"
      frequency: "per_minute"
      command: "Monitor Redis queue length"
      threshold: "< [capacity * 0.8]"
      
    - check: "Job processing time"
      frequency: "per_job"
      command: "Track in APM or Prometheus"
      threshold: "< [constraint from intent]"
      
    - check: "Failed jobs"
      frequency: "hourly"
      command: "Query Celery result backend for failures"
      threshold: "< 5%"
      
  milestone_checks:
    - milestone: "Weekly job health review"
      validation: "Review completion rates, failures, performance trends"
      frequency: "weekly"
      
    - milestone: "Monthly capacity planning"
      validation: "Analyze queue depth trends, worker utilization, scaling needs"
      frequency: "monthly"
      
  monitoring_setup:
    - metric: "job_completion_rate"
      dashboard: "Celery Flower / Grafana - [Job Name] Dashboard"
      alert_threshold: "< [constraint]"
      
    - metric: "job_processing_time_p95"
      dashboard: "APM Dashboard"
      alert_threshold: "> [constraint]"
      
    - metric: "queue_depth"
      dashboard: "Redis Metrics Dashboard"
      alert_threshold: "> [worker_capacity * 0.8]"
      
    - metric: "job_error_rate"
      dashboard: "Error Monitoring Dashboard"
      alert_threshold: "> 5%"

      alert_threshold: "> [worker_capacity * 0.8]"
      
    - metric: "job_error_rate"
      dashboard: "Error Monitoring Dashboard"
      alert_threshold: "> 5%"

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# EXAMPLES
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

examples:
  - name: "Lore Fact Check Job"
    location: "job/fact_checker/generate_lore_fact_check_report_job.py"
    domain: "Gaming / Content Validation"
    customization: |
      - Processes gaming lore articles for fact checking
      - Integrates with fact_checker agent
      - Long-running (can take minutes per article)
      - Idempotent with S3 result caching
    outcome: "95%+ completion rate, < 5 minute p95 processing time"
    link_to_intent: "job/fact_checker/generate_lore_fact_check_report_intent.yaml"

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# BEST PRACTICES
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

best_practices:
  - practice: "Make jobs idempotent"
    why: "Jobs may be retried on failure, must not create duplicate side effects"
    example: "Check if item already processed before processing again"
    
  - practice: "Use exponential backoff for retries"
    why: "Prevents overwhelming system during outages, gives transient issues time to resolve"
    example: "retry_backoff=True in Celery decorator"
    
  - practice: "Distinguish transient vs permanent failures"
    why: "Transient failures should retry, permanent failures shouldn't waste resources"
    example: "autoretry_for=(NetworkError,) but not for ValidationError"
    
  - practice: "Track job metrics"
    why: "Essential for monitoring health, detecting issues, capacity planning"
    example: "Track completion rate, duration, error rate to Prometheus"
    
  - practice: "Set reasonable max_retries"
    why: "Prevents infinite retry loops while allowing recovery from transient issues"
    example: "max_retries=3-5 is typical"
    
  - practice: "Implement progress tracking for long jobs"
    why: "Users/operators need visibility into long-running jobs"
    example: "Update job state: PENDING → PROCESSING → SUCCESS/FAILURE"

common_pitfalls:
  - pitfall: "Jobs are not idempotent"
    symptom: "Duplicate processing, data corruption on retries"
    fix: "Add idempotency checks, use unique identifiers, check if already processed"
    
  - pitfall: "No retry limits"
    symptom: "Jobs retry forever, waste resources, fill logs"
    fix: "Set max_retries (3-5 typical)"
    
  - pitfall: "Retrying permanent failures"
    symptom: "Jobs retry uselessly, slow down queue processing"
    fix: "Distinguish transient vs permanent exceptions, only retry transient"
    
  - pitfall: "No monitoring of job health"
    symptom: "Jobs silently failing, queue backing up, no one notices"
    fix: "Set up metrics, dashboards, alerts on completion rate and queue depth"
    
  - pitfall: "Jobs mutate

: "Add idempotency checks, use unique identifiers, check if already processed"
    
  - pitfall: "No retry limits"
    symptom: "Jobs retry forever, waste resources, fill logs"
    fix: "Set max_retries (3-5 typical)"
    
  - pitfall: "Retrying permanent failures"
    symptom: "Jobs retry uselessly, slow down queue processing"
    fix: "Distinguish transient vs permanent exceptions, only retry transient"
    
  - pitfall: "No monitoring of job health"
    symptom: "Jobs silently failing, queue backing up, no one notices"
    fix: "Set up metrics, dashboards, alerts on completion rate and queue depth"
    
  - pitfall: "Jobs mutate shared state without locking"
    symptom: "Race conditions, data corruption"
    fix: "Use distributed locks (Redis) or design for no shared state"

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# COST & PERFORMANCE
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

typical_costs:
  development_time: "1-3 days (with recipe: 0.5-1 day)"
  infrastructure_cost: "$10-100/month (Redis + worker instances)"
  maintenance_effort: "1-2 hours/month (monitoring, occasional scaling)"

typical_performance:
  latency: "Async (no blocking), processing time varies by job complexity"
  throughput: "10-1000 jobs/minute (depends on worker count and job complexity)"
  reliability: "95-99% completion rate with proper retry logic"

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# REFERENCES
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

references:
  documentation:
    - "your_app/celery_app.py - Celery configuration"
    - "scripts/run_worker.sh - Worker startup script"
    - "Celery Best Practices (Official Docs)"
    
  research:
    - "Distributed Task Queue Patterns (Martin Fowler)"
    - "Idempotency in Distributed Systems"
    
  tools:
    - name: "Celery"
      purpose: "Distributed task queue"
      link: "https://docs.celeryq.dev/"
      
    - name: "Flower"
      purpose: "Celery monitoring web UI"
      link: "https://flower.readthedocs.io/"
      
    - name: "Redis"
      purpose: "Message broker and result backend"
      link: "https://redis.io/"
